{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18817","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18817/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18817/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18817/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18817","id":2527947568,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6WrWsw","number":18817,"title":"sqlite3 error on a local copy of galaxy","user":{"login":"rajkaran27","id":65187424,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY1MTg3NDI0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/65187424?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27","html_url":"https://github.com/rajkaran27","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rajkaran27/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-16T09:29:03Z","updated_at":"2024-09-16T09:29:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.
I cloned and hosted a copy of Galaxy version 24.2 . When I tried running a custom tool, or tried uploading a file, I got this error:
```
(sqlite3.OperationalError)
near
"FROM". syntax error
[SQL:
UPDATE history_dataset_association
SET
info = ?,
update_time=?
FROM dataset
WHERE
history_dataset
```

The SQL query from the update_output_states function seems to be wrong:
```

           UPDATE history_dataset_association
            SET
                info = :info,
                update_time = :update_time
            FROM dataset
            WHERE
                history_dataset_association.dataset_id = dataset.id
                AND dataset.job_id = :job_id;


```
This function is located in /galaxy/packages/data/galaxy/model/__init__.py .

**Galaxy Version**
Galaxy Version: 24.2

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Linux
Browser: Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome

**To Reproduce**
1. [Follow first 4 steps to deploy Galaxy on Linux](https://galaxyproject.org/admin/get-galaxy/)
2. Upload a file of any type.
3. See error

**Expected Output**
The file should be successfully uploaded and accessible for viewing.

**Additional context**
Many proposed solutions recommend migrating to PostgreSQL for production environments. However, this specific error appears to only occur in the current version of Galaxy.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18817/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18817/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
